title: Shipyard workflow worker return
description: Structured result a /shipyard:do-work worker returns when dispatched through the Dynamic Workflows substrate (dispatch.substrate == "workflow"). This is the schema-validated replacement for the free-text return-string convention documented in skills/worker-preamble/SKILL.md § "Return-contract discipline" — the workflow runtime validates each agent()/pipeline() stage result against this schema (agent(prompt, { schema }) in a Dynamic Workflow), so a malformed or ambiguous return fails loudly at the stage boundary instead of being re-parsed from prose by the orchestrator. Phase 1 of the #782 epic (issue #787) DEFINES and documents this schema; it is NOT yet consumed by any live dispatch (dispatch.substrate defaults to "agent", and no worker mode is wired to the workflow script). The field set is a 1:1 structured encoding of the return-string vocabulary each per-mode file (agents/issue-worker/<mode>.md) documents — outcome + the same qualifiers the free-text suffixes carry.

mode.description: The worker mode this result came from — the same value the dispatch prompt names as `mode:` (agents/issue-worker.md routing table). Lets the orchestrator's reconcile validate that the structured result's shape matches the mode it dispatched.

outcome.description: Terminal outcome class, mapping onto the free-text return-string leading token. `shipped` — issue-work opened a PR (see auto_merge). `green` — fix-checks-only drove an existing PR's checks green. `rebased` — fix-rebase rebased a DIRTY PR onto the default branch. `noop` — a fix mode found nothing to do (e.g. the failing run resolved itself between dispatch and read). `blocked` — a real blocker was hit before completion (see blocked_reason); the orchestrator's step-A reconcile classifies it into needs-human-review / blocked:agent-soft / no-label per #521. `reaped` — the worktree was reaped mid-run; RETRYABLE, re-enqueued with no block label (distinct from `blocked`). `disposition` — investigate/spike mode dispositioned the issue without a PR (see disposition).

issue.description: The issue number this dispatch targeted (#N). Null for synthetic diverts with no originating issue (fix-main-ci, fix-failing-prs-batch).

pr.description: The PR number this dispatch opened or acted on (#M). Present on `shipped` / `green` / `rebased`; null on `noop` / `blocked` / `reaped` / a no-PR `disposition`.

auto_merge.description: For `shipped` (issue-work), the auto-merge outcome — the structured form of the return-string `(auto-merge: …)` suffix. `enabled`: --auto armed, queued behind checks. `gated-manual`: the ungated-admin-direct-merge pre-check (#598/#716) fired, worker watched checks and merged by hand. `merged-direct`: --auto fell through to an immediate merge with checks green at merge time. `merged-direct-ungated`: same, but the PR landed before CI completed (no required checks — #457). `unavailable`: the merge call genuinely failed, needs manual merge. `gated-external`: external-author origin — auto-merge intentionally skipped, needs-human-review applied. Null for non-issue-work modes.

checks.description: One-shot snapshot of the PR's check rollup at return time (latest run per check name, per #333) — the structured form of the return-string `checks: …` suffix. NEVER a wait: the worker returns on green|pending|failing alike (#707). Null when no PR is involved.

disposition.description: For `disposition` outcomes from investigate / spike mode: how the issue was dispositioned. `fix` — rewrote into a real spec and opened a fixing PR (pr is then set). `needs-human-review` — handed to the human queue. `auto-close-noise` — closed as non-actionable noise. `duplicate` — closed as a duplicate. `decomposed` — (spike) sharded into a sub-issue chain. `designed` — (spike) produced a design doc / decision. Null for code-worker modes.

blocked_reason.description: For `blocked` outcomes, the human-readable reason (the text after `blocked:` in the return string). Load-bearing for the orchestrator's reconcile: a reason naming an open `Blocked by #N` is a dependency-wait (no label); anything else is a refuse (needs-human-review). Null unless outcome == "blocked".

blocked_stage.description: Optional stage annotation for a `blocked` outcome (e.g. "branch-setup", "pre-pr-create", "pre-push", "closing-link-verify") mirroring the `blocked #<N> at <stage>:` free-text form. Null when not applicable.

last_push.description: For `reaped` outcomes, the short hash of the last commit the worker pushed before its worktree was reaped (or null / "none" if it never pushed). Mirrors the `(last push: <hash|none>)` suffix so a re-dispatch can tell whether any work survived on the remote.

summary.description: Optional one-line human-readable summary for the orchestrator's end-of-session report. Not consumed by reconcile logic — provenance only.

allOf.$comment: A blocked outcome must carry a reason.
A disposition outcome must name how it dispositioned.
